Romance Dawn: Monkey D. Luffy sets out on an adventure to form a crew, find the legendary One Piece, and become the pirate king.
Orange Town: Luffy and Zoro run afoul of a flashy crew of pirates and their captain, Buggy the Clown. They are joined by a young girl named Nami.
Syrup Village: Luffy's fledgling pirate crew arrives at the slopes of Syrup Village. There they find a liar and a conspiracy to steal a young lady's fortune.
Gaimon: With Usopp now on the crew and their new ship, the Going Merry, under their command, the Straw Hats head to an island said to hold fabled treasure.
Baratie: The Straw Hats head to the ocean-going restaurant, Baratie, with hopes of recruiting one of their cooks. Hungry yet powerful forces gather to lay siege.
Arlong Park: Nami's hometown has long been overrun by Arlong and his band of fishmen from the Grand Line. Nami has left out key details about herself.
The Adventures of Buggy's Crew: Buggy, after being defeated by Luffy, Zoro, and Nami, begins his quest to regain his body parts and crew, then get his flashy revenge.
The Adventures of Buggys Crew: Buggy, after being defeated by Luffy, Zoro, and Nami, begins his quest to regain his body parts and crew, then get his flashy revenge.
Loguetown: The final island before entering the Grand Line: Loguetown. Luffy and his crew drop anchor to stock up, sightsee, and pay respects to Gold Roger's last breath.
Reverse Mountain: Reverse Mountain is the entrance to the Grand Line. Impossible water currents, a giant whale, and dubious people going by codenames await.
Whiskey Peak: The first island on the Grand Line is surprisingly welcoming to pirates. But as night falls, a deadly plot unravels.
Whisky Peak: The first island on the Grand Line is surprisingly welcoming to pirates. But as night falls, a deadly plot unravels.
The Trials of Koby-Meppo: Koby and Helmeppo are new cabin boys for the marines, becoming unwillingly involved in an escape attempt by Morgan. Their bravery earns respect.
Little Garden: The Straw Hats vow to bring Princess Vivi to Arabasta, encountering Little Garden, an island from prehistoria with creatures and people larger than life.
Drum Island: When Nami falls ill, the crew makes a detour from Arabasta to seek medical help on a winter island with a mysterious doctor and reindeer.
Arabasta: Arabasta teeters on the edge of civil war. Princess Vivi and the Straw Hats must stem the rebellion, uncover Baroque Works' plot, and beat Crocodile.
Alabasta: Arabasta teeters on the edge of civil war. Princess Vivi and the Straw Hats must stem the rebellion, uncover Baroque Works' plot, and beat Crocodile.
Jaya: The Straw Hats seek the fabled sky island Skypiea and head to Jaya for information. Their convictions are tested by pirates of Mock Town.
Skypiea: Arriving at Skypiea, the Straw Hats encounter unfamiliar sights and customs. The god of the sky island deems Blue Sea dwellers trespassers.
Long Ring Long Land: On Long Ring Long Land, the Straw Hats are challenged to a Davy Back Fight with serious outcomes. The Navy pays closer attention to crew members.
Water Seven: The Straw Hats make landfall at Water Seven, known for unparalleled shipyards. The city threatens to take their money, ship, and crewmates.
Enies Lobby: To free Nico Robin from the World Government, the Straw Hats and allies invade the judicial island and take on the government's CP9 unit.
Post-Enies Lobby: The Straw Hats return to Water Seven after Enies Lobby. They are met by a familial vice admiral and await news of their new ship.
Thriller Bark: The Straw Hats must traverse the dreaded Florian Triangle with disappearing crews, conversational skeletons, and a floating island of zombies and horrors.
Sabaody Archipelago: The second half of the Grand Line awaits. The Straw Hats come face-to-face with the World Government's darkest forces near the Holy Land.
Amazon Lily: Luffy is separated from his crew on an unfamiliar island in the Calm Belt—an island inhabited only by women where men are prohibited.
Impel Down: Luffy launches a daring rescue mission to free Ace from the government prison Impel Down. It's a race through hell itself to the lowest levels.
The Adventures of the Straw Hats: Sanji resists; Robin meets terrible people. Franky, Usopp, Chopper, Nami, Brook, and Zoro experience separate adventures during crew separation.
The Adventures of the Straw Hat: Sanji resists; Robin meets terrible people. Franky, Usopp, Chopper, Nami, Brook, and Zoro experience separate adventures during crew separation.
Marineford: Whitebeard and allies versus the Navy and Seven Warlords. Portgas D. Ace's past is revealed as Luffy races to his execution scaffold.
Post-War: The war has taken its toll. Luffy reminisces about meeting Ace as a child. He makes a critical decision for himself and his crew's future.
Return to Sabaody: Two years have passed since the Straw Hats were forcibly separated. They reconvene at Sabaody, stronger and more committed to their journey.
Fishman Island: The path to the New World takes the crew thousands of leagues under the sea to underwater Fishman Island. The island's dark past reaches a breaking point.
Punk Hazard: The first island of the New World is strange—flames on one half, freezing temperatures on the other—revealing a laboratory and maniac scientist.
Dressrosa: Luffy's alliance with Law brings him to Dressrosa to take down Doflamingo's booming business. Doflamingo has control over everything.
Zou: Zou cannot be reached by conventional means. Within its secretive walls lies the Mink Tribe, holding pieces of the puzzle that unlock world mysteries.
Whole Cake Island: Luffy stubbornly goes after Sanji to Whole Cake Island to not sail without his cook. Knocking on an emperor's door is easier said than done.
Reverie: Every four years, world leaders gather at the Holy Land to discuss a mutually agreed upon government path. Many parties with agendas arrive, some uninvited.
Wano: The Straw Hats finally regroup in Wano to carry out their ultimate goal: Emperor Kaido's downfall and restoring the Kozuki family to rightful power.
Egghead: Futuristic tech, giant alien monsters, battle robots, superhuman clones appear. Multiple Dr. Vegapunk identities raise questions about government pursuit.
